steps.title: Recognize the complex series
steps.content: The sentence lists three parts of Buchi Emecheta's oeuvre. The first item is The Joys of Motherhood, followed by a descriptive phrase about a novel set in 1950s Lagos. The second item is A Kind of Marriage, followed by a descriptive phrase about a television play. The third item is Head Above Water, followed by a descriptive phrase identifying it as her autobiography. Because the items themselves contain commas, semicolons should separate the larger list items.

steps.title: Punctuate the second item
steps.content: Choice A uses only a comma after Lagos, which is not strong enough to separate the first large item from the second in this complex series. Choice C also uses only a comma after Lagos and then adds an unsuitable colon after the title. Choice D correctly uses a semicolon after Lagos but lacks the comma after A Kind of Marriage that introduces the description "a television play." The remaining choice uses both marks correctly: a semicolon between list items and a comma after the second title. The answer is B.
